message_text: I do want to clear this loan, but I cannot pay the full amount today.

I’m an AI agent acting on behalf of Nira, and this conversation is being logged. I’m contacting you about your Nira loan ending ****4831. To verify I’m speaking with the right person, please confirm the last four digits of the account reference.
Thank you. Your current amount due is 12,921. I also note hardship signals on the account. I’m completing the assessment and passing this to the next agent to review policy-allowed options.

Thanks. I had an unexpected family medical expense and my salary is delayed, but I can make a small payment now and a larger one after my next salary date if that works.

Given the medical expense and delayed salary, this should go through hardship-first review. Under current Nira policy, any payment arrangement must complete within 30 days, and I cannot confirm exact approved reduced-closure or plan terms from the available data in this turn. Based on what is already on file, if your larger payment can complete the remaining balance by 2026-04-20, I can record that hardship-based request for lender review. If that timing no longer works, give one date within the 30-day window that does.
Yes, that timing can work. I can make a small payment today and clear the remaining balance by 2026-04-20, please record that for review.

I’ve recorded your hardship-based request for review: a small payment today and the remaining balance by 2026-04-20. That timing fits the current 30-day policy window, but I cannot confirm exact approved plan or reduced-closure terms from the available data in this turn. You asked for review, so I’ve noted that. Because you asked to record this for review rather than accept a confirmed approved arrangement, no arrangement is finalized yet.
